writing_tasks.title: Writing Task 1 — Line graph: library visits
writing_tasks.prompt:
The line graph below shows the average number of monthly visits to three public libraries in a city from 2018 to 2023.

Average monthly visits (thousands):
Central Library: 2018 42, 2019 45, 2020 31, 2021 36, 2022 49, 2023 58
Riverside Library: 2018 18, 2019 22, 2020 25, 2021 29, 2022 34, 2023 41
Hilltop Library: 2018 27, 2019 26, 2020 20, 2021 22, 2022 24, 2023 23

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.

writing_tasks.title: Writing Task 1 — Bar chart: commuting methods
writing_tasks.prompt:
The bar chart below compares the main commuting methods used by workers in two districts in 2024.

Share of workers (%):
Car: Northbank 38, Eastmere 24
Bus: Northbank 22, Eastmere 31
Train: Northbank 16, Eastmere 27
Bicycle: Northbank 14, Eastmere 8
Walking: Northbank 10, Eastmere 10

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.

writing_tasks.title: Writing Task 1 — Table: household energy use
writing_tasks.prompt:
The table below shows average household energy use in four categories in two years.

Average annual energy use per household (kWh):
Heating: 2014 6800, 2024 5200
Water heating: 2014 2500, 2024 2200
Appliances: 2014 1900, 2024 2600
Lighting: 2014 900, 2024 450

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.

writing_tasks.title: Writing Task 1 — Pie charts: university spending
writing_tasks.prompt:
The pie charts below show how a university allocated its annual budget in 2015 and 2025.

Budget share (%):
Teaching staff: 2015 42, 2025 36
Research: 2015 18, 2025 25
Buildings and maintenance: 2015 16, 2025 14
Technology: 2015 9, 2025 17
Student services: 2015 15, 2025 8

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.

writing_tasks.title: Writing Task 1 — Process: recycled glass bottles
writing_tasks.prompt:
The diagram below describes the process by which used glass bottles are recycled into new bottles.

Stages:
1. Used bottles are deposited in colour-coded collection bins.
2. Trucks transport the bottles to a recycling plant.
3. Bottles are sorted by colour and unsuitable items are removed.
4. The glass is washed and crushed into small pieces called cullet.
5. Cullet is melted in a furnace at high temperature.
6. The molten glass is moulded into new bottles.
7. The bottles are cooled, inspected, filled, and delivered to shops.

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features.

Write at least 150 words.

writing_tasks.title: Writing Task 1 — Map: town centre redevelopment
writing_tasks.prompt:
The maps below show changes planned for a town centre between the present day and 2030.

Present town centre:
- A large car park occupies the north-west corner.
- A bus station is located in the north-east corner.
- Small shops line both sides of Market Street.
- The public library stands south of Market Street.
- A narrow footpath runs along the river in the south.

Planned town centre in 2030:
- The car park will be replaced by an apartment block and a small public square.
- The bus station will move next to the railway station east of the centre.
- Market Street will become pedestrian-only.
- The library will be extended and connected to a new community hall.
- The riverside footpath will be widened and linked to a cycle lane.

Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

Write at least 150 words.
